paper_title: Isolation and Analysis of Pollen Grains

methods.description: This method describes the process of isolating pollen grains from Tradescantia paludosa, preparing them for incubation with radioactive thymidine, and fixing them for further analysis. The purpose is to obtain clean pollen grains free from contaminants for accurate experimental results.

steps.description: Buds were removed from an inflorescence, surface-sterilized by dipping in 10 per cent Clorox, then rinsed in distilled water and one anther from each bud sampled to determine the stage.
steps.searchable_summary: Surface sterilize and rinse buds, sample anther to determine stage

steps.description: Five anthers from a bud were homogenized with a glass rod in a 10 X 75 mm glass test tube in 0.1 mi of 0.1 M phosphate buffer pH 7.0 containing 0.25 M sucrose (designated SPB).
steps.searchable_summary: Homogenize anthers in phosphate buffer with sucrose

steps.description: The homogenate was centrifuged for 30 seconds to a maximum 3000 RPM in a clinical centrifuge and the supernatant decanted.
The twice-washed pellet was resuspended in the SPB incubation medium to which radioactive thymidine, ca. 1 c/ml, was added.
steps.searchable_summary: Resuspend pellet in SPB with radioactive thymidine

steps.description: One ml aliquots of the suspension, containing 7-8 X 103 pollen grains, were transferred to 10 ml Erlenmeyer flasks; the flasks were capped with aluminum foil and incubated at 25°C in a Dubnoff shaker at 60 cycles/min.
steps.searchable_summary: Incubate pollen grain suspension in Erlenmeyer flasks

steps.description: The pollen grains were harvested by centrifuging off the cold incubation medium, washing once with ice cold SPB, and to the washed pellet adding one ml of ice cold 3:1 ethanol: acetic acid.
steps.searchable_summary: Harvest and fix pollen grains with ethanol:acetic acid

methods.description: This method involves staining the pollen grains using the Feulgen procedure and preparing them for autoradiography to visualize nuclear orientation and thymidine uptake.

steps.description: Small aliquots of the water suspension were pipetted onto slides subbed with gelatin/chrome alum, and coverslips applied.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare slides with pollen grain suspension

steps.description: After removal of the coverslips with dry ice, the pollen grains were hydrolysed on the slides for 10 minutes at 60°C in 1 N HCI, stained by the Feulgen procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: Hydrolyze and stain pollen grains using Feulgen procedure

steps.description: The stained slides were then dipped into Kodak NTB2 liquid emulsion, undiluted, at 40°C. Exposure generally was for 1 week.
steps.searchable_summary: Dip slides in Kodak NTB2 emulsion for autoradiography

steps.description: The emulsion was developed in Dektol for 2 minutes at 18°C and the autoradiographs dehydrated and mounted permanently with Euparal.
